title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. fuchsii
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. fuchsii, also known as the spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and western Asia. It is a type of wild orchid that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant contains iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it beneficial for relieving congestion and coughs. Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. fuchsii can be prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
